seoTitle: How to Create a Newsletter in Brevo
metaDescription: Design and send a professional newsletter in Brevo using the drag-and-drop builder, brand elements, secure links, and scheduled delivery windows.
intro: Building a professional audience newsletter shouldn't mean wrestling with rigid layouts or writing custom CSS. Brevo's drag-and-drop builder handles responsive formatting automatically, letting you focus on the content and branding. This guide covers setting up your campaign and designing a layout that renders well across devices.

steps.heading: Set your campaign foundations
steps.body: Input a clear subject line, preview snippet, and verified sender alias before starting on the visual design.

steps.heading: Design your visual grid
steps.body: Use the sidebar library to drag and drop media, text blocks, and structured multi-column layouts onto your canvas.

steps.heading: Apply brand elements
steps.body: Configure custom brand colors, logos, and fonts so the newsletter matches your existing visual identity.

steps.heading: Map secure link paths
steps.body: Add and verify the destination links for any call-to-action buttons or article previews included in the newsletter.

steps.heading: Schedule delivery and verify rendering
steps.body: Set your delivery window and double-check the layout renders correctly before the newsletter goes out.

faq.question: Do I need coding knowledge to build a newsletter in Brevo?
faq.answer: No, the drag-and-drop builder is designed for no-code use, handling responsive formatting automatically.

faq.question: Can I reuse the same newsletter layout each month?
faq.answer: Yes, you can save your design as a template and reuse the structure for future editions.

faq.question: How do I make sure the newsletter looks good on mobile?
faq.answer: Brevo's editor automatically applies responsive layout structures, but it's still worth previewing on mobile before sending.

faq.question: Can I schedule a newsletter to send at a specific time?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set a specific delivery window rather than sending immediately.
